common_mistakes.consequence: Coaches routinely misjudge readiness; re-sprain risk spikes
common_mistakes.symptom: Returning from sprain too early — swelling resolves before proprioceptive capacity does

key_principles.text: Proprioception is housed in ligament mechanoreceptors — every sprain degrades balance and rapid-adjustment for weeks after the acute injury heals.
Proactive proprioceptive training is the single highest-value injury-prevention investment for basketball.

common_mistakes.consequence: Spinal stability fails under contact; plank, Pallof-press, bird-dog fill this gap

key_principles.text: Rectus abdominis + obliques + erector spinae produce and resist spinal movement in multiple planes — the engine of every trunk-driven basketball action.
"Draw in the abdominals" first — inner-core activation precedes outer-core loading; without it, outer-core work leaks stability.

common_mistakes.consequence: Underdeveloped posterior chain; hamstring overuse and strain risk
common_mistakes.symptom: Quadriceps-dominant training only (squats with hip-flexor bias)

key_principles.text: Largest muscle in the body — greatest force-production capacity; training it lifts every explosive basketball action.
Horizontal and vertical propulsion — hip thrust for horizontal (driving past), squat for vertical (jumping).

common_mistakes.consequence: Tight, short, weak muscles prone to strain on explosive cuts
common_mistakes.symptom: Flexion strength only (leg raises, sit-ups) without mobility work

key_principles.text: Mobility before strength — a tight iliopsoas limits stride length and baseline-cut depth before strength ever becomes the limiter.
Reciprocal with the glutes — chronically tight hip flexors inhibit gluteal activation; training mobility here unlocks the posterior chain.

drill-ankling.coaching_cue: Quiet but fast feet — loud landings indicate too much ground contact time; emphasize the push-off, not just the landing.

primary_form_error.consequence: Breaks the ankle's elastic rhythm; defeats the rapid-contact target

drill-back-extension.coaching_cue: Hip hinge only — all motion comes from the hip joint; the upper torso stays stable and rigid.

primary_form_error.consequence: Reduces target loading; if seen, shorten the range of motion

safety_tip.text: Do not go beyond parallel to the floor or arch the spine — hyperextension places dangerous stress on the vertebrae.

drill-band-lateral-walk.coaching_cue: Stay in the squat — don't stand up between steps; keep tension on the band at all times.

primary_form_error.consequence: Takes tension off the lateral hip; defeats the propulsion target

drill-bird-dog.coaching_cue: "Carpenter's level" — a level across the shoulders and across the hips must stay perfectly flat; no tilting.

primary_form_error.consequence: Most common error; reduce hold time before adding reps
primary_form_error.symptom: Hip or shoulder rotation during arm/leg extension

exercise-hip-thrust.coaching_cue: Drive through the hips, not the lower back — extension comes from the hips, not the low back.

primary_form_error.consequence: Transfers load off the glute and into the lower back

safety_tip.text: Back of shoulder firm on padded bench; cervical spine (neck) must NOT be the main support.
